title: Coelogyne sulawesiensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne sulawesiensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which consists of over 200 species of orchids. Within the genus, Coelogyne sulawesiensis is a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and geographical distribution. The exact classification of Coelogyne sulawesiensis is Coelogyne sulawesiensis (Schuit.) J.J.Wood & P.T.Ong, which reflects its taxonomic position. This classification highlights the species' position within the Orchidaceae family and its specific place within the Coelogyne genus.

intro: Coelogyne sulawesiensis is a species of orchid, specifically a terrestrial epiphyte native to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. Bioactive constituents of Coelogyne sulawesiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
